heading: Partition action where United States is joint tenant
label: 1347 Partition action where United States is joint tenant
text: The district courts shall have original jurisdiction of any civil action commenced by any tenant in common or joint tenant for the partition of lands where the United States is one of the tenants in common or joint tenants.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 41(25) (, par. 25, ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 2436 Stat. 1094The venue provision in , U.S.C., 1940 ed., is incorporated in .section 41(25) of title 28section 1399 of this titleWords “civil action” were substituted for “suits in equity,” in view of Rule 2 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.A change was made in phraseology.
